summary: Extract seed and bootstrap logic out of server routes
problem_statement: Seed data and bootstrap logic are currently embedded inside the route registration module (server/routes.ts). This conflates two concerns — route definitions and data initialisation — making the routes file harder to read, test, and maintain. Extracting seed logic into a dedicated module isolates the concern and simplifies the routes file.
in_scope: Identify all seed data and bootstrap/initialisation logic currently inside server/routes.ts
Extract that logic into a dedicated seed or bootstrap module (e.g. server/seed.ts or server/bootstrap.ts)
Update server startup to invoke the extracted module at the appropriate point
Ensure the extracted module preserves the exact same seeding behaviour and data
out_of_scope: Changing the seed data itself or the conditions under which seeding runs
Database schema changes — the existing Drizzle schema and migrations remain unchanged
Adding new seed data or a seed management framework
Refactoring route handlers beyond removing seed logic — route cleanup is handled by other tasks
requirements: All seed/bootstrap logic is removed from server/routes.ts and placed in a dedicated module
The dedicated module is invoked during server startup and produces the same result as the original inline logic
server/routes.ts contains only route registration after extraction
acceptance_criteria: server/routes.ts no longer contains seed data or bootstrap logic
A dedicated seed/bootstrap module exists and is invoked during server startup
Application behaviour at startup is unchanged — same data is seeded under the same conditions
npm run check passes with no new type errors introduced
dependencies: P2-R2-07 — storage layer contracts should be typed so the extracted module can reference typed storage methods
risks: Seed logic may be tightly coupled to route registration timing (e.g. runs after certain middleware) — extraction must preserve execution order
implementation_notes: Keep behaviour unchanged. The extracted module should export a function (e.g. seedDatabase or bootstrap) that accepts the storage instance and runs the existing seed logic. Call it from the server entry point (server/index.ts or equivalent) after storage is initialised but before routes are registered.
